2020-05-01.title: Hundreds of Rohingya Refugees Stuck at Sea With ‘Zero Hope’
2020-05-01.abstract: At least three boats carrying Rohingya refugees have been adrift for more than two months. As of this week, rights groups that had been tracking the boats lost sight of them.

2020-05-01.abstract: Twenty percent of the staff at one hospital is quarantined, but doors remain open as the war wounded are brought in.

2020-05-04.abstract: Moscow long rejected OPEC’s pleas to reduce output, citing its frosty climate as an excuse. Now, facing a glut, the country is closing wells, many in the Arctic.

2020-05-25.abstract: When the rattling from the magnitude-5.8 quake stopped, New Zealand’s prime minister continued the interview, seeming unruffled.

2020-05-25.title: As the U.S. Honors Memorial Day, Trump Threatens to Move Republican Convention
2020-05-25.abstract: The president threatened to yank the Republican National Convention from Charlotte, N.C. Meatpacking plants are reopening, but data about how many workers are ill is elusive.

2020-05-25.title: By Air and Sea, Mercenaries Landed in Libya. Then the Plan Went South.
2020-05-25.abstract: A short-lived mission uncovered by U.N. investigators offers a glimpse into the world of those who have thrived off Libya’s chaos.

2020-05-25.title: Spain’s Courts, Already Strained, Face Crisis as Lockdown Lifts
2020-05-25.abstract: A country known for litigiousness has gingerly restarted court proceedings, and lawyers are bracing for chaos.

2020-05-26.title: 2 Weeks, 6.5 Million Coronavirus Tests as Wuhan Nears Goal
2020-05-26.abstract: The Chinese city where the outbreak began is seeking to test all its 11 million residents, and the pandemic has forced the fashion industry to take a hard look in the mirror.

2020-05-26.abstract: The commander of the People’s Liberation Army garrison in Hong Kong and the city’s leader defended Beijing’s push for new national security laws governing the territory.

2020-05-26.title: How the Taliban Outlasted a Superpower: Tenacity and Carnage
2020-05-26.abstract: The Taliban stand on the brink of realizing their most fervent desire: U.S. troops leaving Afghanistan. They have given up little of their extremist ideology to do it.

2020-05-26.title: Here’s How Wuhan Tested 6.5 Million for Coronavirus in Days
2020-05-26.abstract: The Wuhan government is close to completing its citywide testing drive. Thousands of medical and other workers were mobilized in a feat that some health experts and residents questioned.

2020-05-26.title: Visiting My Grandparents, Separated by Plexiglass
2020-05-26.abstract: With a partition in front of him, Elian Peltier, a New York Times reporter, experienced a strange new reality when he checked in on his relations at a nursing home outside Paris.

2020-05-27.title: France Bans Malaria Drug for Coronavirus Treatments
2020-05-27.abstract: Nissan and Renault and trying to reset their troubled relationship. An E.U. recovery plan could bring the bloc closer to a shared budget.

2020-05-27.title: Canada’s ‘Voice of the Nation’ Rings Out in a Capital Quieted by a Virus
2020-05-27.abstract: In a belfry high above Ottawa, the Dominion Carillonneur performs live music for a tiny audience, chiming 53 bells to help keep up morale during a health crisis.

2020-05-27.title: This Chef Has a Michelin Star and a Mission: Feeding Millions in India’s Lockdown
2020-05-27.abstract: From his home in New York, Vikas Khanna, the TV host, writer and former executive chef at Junoon, is running a relief effort that distributes food to vulnerable Indians.

2020-05-27.abstract: Many sought to oppose a bill that would threaten prison for disrespecting the Chinese national anthem, the latest expression of public anger at Beijing’s encroachment.

2020-05-27.title: ‘Overtaken by Aliens’: India Faces Another Plague as Locusts Swarm
2020-05-27.abstract: Blizzards of bugs are descending on India at an already tough time. Scientists say climate change is making the infestation worse.

2020-05-28.title: Coronavirus Spread Speeds Up, Even as Nations Reopen
2020-05-28.abstract: In just the last week, even as some countries move to reopen, 700,000 new infections have been reported as the virus takes firmer hold in Latin America and the Middle East. The Boston Marathon has been canceled for 2020.

2020-05-28.abstract: Beijing ordered that a new law be written to extend many of mainland China’s security practices to Hong Kong, creating broad powers to quash unrest.

2020-05-28.title: ‘It’s Not the Virus’: Mexico’s Broken Hospitals Become Killers, Too
2020-05-28.abstract: Years of neglect have hobbled many Mexican hospitals. Now, as the pandemic strikes, some patients are dying from neglect or from mistakes that are easily prevented, doctors and nurses say.

2020-05-28.title: Proud of Your Lockdown Baking? This Man Built a Giant Kookaburra
2020-05-28.abstract: He simply wanted to give people a laugh.

2020-05-28.title: ‘It’s Too Late’: In Sprawling Indonesia, Coronavirus Surges
2020-05-28.abstract: A random sampling of thousands found that one in 10 had antibodies for the coronavirus, an alarming glimpse at what could be runaway transmission.

2020-05-29.title: India Loosens Restrictions, Despite Coronavirus Surge
2020-05-29.abstract: Baghdad has shut down again; Israel might as well. Moscow has doubled its official death toll.

2020-05-29.title: The Return to School
2020-05-29.abstract: What Australian children have to say about going back to class

2020-05-29.title: Its Coronavirus Caseload Soaring, India Is Reopening Anyway
2020-05-29.abstract: Worried for its economy, India loosened tough restrictions and may lift them entirely this weekend. But infection numbers are climbing fast.

2020-05-29.title: In a German Restaurant, the Sommelier Lifts His Mask to Smell the Wine
2020-05-29.abstract: Pauly Saal, a Michelin-starred restaurant in Berlin, was one of the first restaurants in Europe to restart operations last week. How did its chefs, waiters — and diners — cope on its opening night?

2020-05-29.abstract: When it left Vietnam in 1973, the South Korean military said none of its soldiers had been taken prisoner. It took Ahn Yong-soo decades to change the narrative.

2020-05-30.abstract: As the Chinese government’s grip tightens, protesters are debating how and why to keep fighting, or whether resistance is even possible.

2020-05-30.abstract: The German chancellor had announced earlier Saturday that she would not travel to the U.S. for the G7. Businesses are starting up again, but workers commuting by public transportation face dilemmas.

2020-05-30.title: In Hong Kong, Anxiety and Defiance Over Trump’s Move to Cut Ties
2020-05-30.abstract: The reactions to President Trump’s decision fell along familiar political fault lines in a city wracked by nearly a year of protests.

2020-05-30.title: A Mile-Long Line for Free Food in Geneva, One of World’s Richest Cities
2020-05-30.abstract: The Swiss city is best known for bankers, watchmakers and U.N. officials. But the virus has forced thousands from Geneva’s underclass to line up for hours for food aid.

2020-05-30.abstract: Lacking equipment, expertise and authority, a divided, war-torn country bobbles a response to a surging pandemic.

2020-05-31.abstract: The two countries are studying whether the drug is safe and effective for the prevention and early treatment of Covid-19. Nicaragua has become a place of midnight burials.

2020-05-31.abstract: The children are living in squalid detention camps lacking schools, health care and clean water, and where disease and jihadist ideology are rampant.

2020-05-31.abstract: The leader compared the requests for help to prank calls, the latest controversy over his government’s response to record levels of violence against women.

2020-05-31.title: The Drive-In Theater: Keeping Drama Alive During the Lockdown
2020-05-31.abstract: Czech theater companies couldn’t perform onstage during the early phases of the pandemic. So they took over a parking lot.

2020-05-31.abstract: With high-end restaurants closed, the price of prawns has tumbled, allowing fishmongers to pick up the shellfish at a discount and offer them to a much broader clientele.
